articles.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation after cargo ship struck; US blames Iranian drone
articles.summary: A cargo vessel was hit on its starboard side by an 'unknown projectile' about 7.5 nautical miles off the coast of Oman, damaging the bridge, with no casualties reported. The International Maritime Organization paused its plan to evacuate ships stranded around the Strait of Hormuz, saying the initiative would stay on hold until safety guarantees could be confirmed. A US official said the ship was struck by an Iranian drone; Iran did not claim responsibility but the attack came hours after the IRGC warned that vessels would only be granted safe passage via Iranian-designated routes. It was the first reported attack since the US and Iran agreed last week to work toward a peace deal.

articles.title: Iran declares new Hormuz route 'unacceptable and dangerous', warns ships against unauthorized passage
articles.summary: Iran on June 25 described the alternative Strait of Hormuz transit route as 'unacceptable and dangerous' and warned vessels against crossing without Tehran's clearance. The IRGC said safe passage would only be guaranteed along Iranian-approved corridors, reviving tensions over Tehran's demand for a say over Hormuz traffic and reported toll proposals. Gulf states and the US have rejected any Iranian fees on shipping through the waterway, which carries roughly a fifth of global oil supply.

articles.title: Oil prices spike as much as 4% then ease after Hormuz evacuation halt
articles.summary: Brent crude rose as much as 4 percent after the IMO suspended its Hormuz evacuation plan following the attack on a cargo ship, before paring gains and falling more than 2 percent on Friday. Brent had climbed near $74.70 a barrel as traders monitored shipments through the strait. Prices moderated as growing confidence in a lasting US-Iran agreement encouraged more tankers to transit with tracking signals switched on, though levels remained near pre-war marks.

articles.title: Gulf states back US-Iran deal as analysts say Israel's war backfired
articles.summary: All six Gulf Cooperation Council states welcomed the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, a stance analysts attributed to hard-headed pragmatism and a desire to end the war rather than newfound trust in Tehran. The GCC statement condemned attacks on member states by Iranian proxies in Iraq and urged Baghdad to curb Iranian influence, while keeping focus on Hormuz security and a proposed regional investment fund for Iran. Commentators framed the alignment as a setback for Israel, whose campaign against Iran did not translate into Gulf backing for its position.

articles.title: Gaza civil-military coordination hub to be overhauled and renamed International Gaza Aid Center
articles.summary: The Civil-Military Coordination Center in Kiryat Gat is set to be overhauled to make it more operational and tie it more closely to US President Trump's Board of Peace, according to three diplomats, and will be renamed the International Gaza Aid Center. The body monitors the Israel-Hamas ceasefire, facilitates aid entry and develops postwar policy. Separately, Georgia and Vietnam were reported to be in talks to contribute troops to the planned International Stabilization Force for Gaza.

articles.title: Israeli strike near Nabatieh kills three in south Lebanon despite ceasefire
articles.summary: Lebanon's health ministry said an Israeli strike on a car in Mayfadoun, near Nabatieh, on June 25 killed three people and wounded one, according to a statement carried by Lebanon's National News Agency. The strike came amid a fragile ceasefire and continuing Israeli operations in the south, which Israel says target Hezbollah operatives. Defense Minister Israel Katz reiterated that Israeli forces would remain in security zones in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza 'without any time limit'.

articles.title: Palestinian man killed in Israeli raid near Salfit in occupied West Bank
articles.summary: A 32-year-old Palestinian man was killed by Israeli forces during a raid on his home near the town of Salfit in the occupied West Bank on June 25. The killing came amid a broader rise in settler violence, with prosecutors indicting a former Yitzhar council head over allegations he threatened Palestinian drivers in Huwara with an army-issued rifle during a settler attack. Rights groups continue to report escalating raids, demolitions and settler attacks across the territory.

articles.title: US-Iran deconfliction channel to base IRGC and CENTCOM officers in Qatar, Vance says
articles.summary: US Vice President JD Vance said the deconfliction channel agreed during talks in Switzerland will include representatives of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and the US Army's Central Command, both based in Qatar. The mechanism is intended to prevent miscommunication and renewed clashes as Washington and Tehran work toward a final deal within 60 days. The announcement came alongside continued friction over the Strait of Hormuz and unresolved questions on Iran's nuclear program.
